title: Cardboard Castle
central_metaphor: A childhood fort built from cardboard boxes, representing the self-contained, imaginative world of childhood that feels grand and real, contrasting with the hollow, complex "kingdoms" of adulthood.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The gravel road was a dusty chalk line
To a finish tape of rusty chain-link fence
We’d pedal hard till the summer sun declined
And measure time in popsicle-stick expense
[Pre-Chorus]
The world was small as a paper map we drew
And every path led back to that front yard
[Chorus]
We were kings of a cardboard castle
Rulers of a two-wheeled steed
Our treasury was bottle-cap jewels
And a loyalty we didn’t need
[Verse 2]
Now my kingdom’s a commute on a concrete track
And the crown I wear’s a weight I can’t adjust
I drive right past where the old fence used to crack
Through a ghost town made of mortgage dust
[Pre-Chorus]
The world got big as a highway I can’t chart
And every path just leads me further from that yard
[Chorus]
We were kings of a cardboard castle
Rulers of a two-wheeled steed
Our treasury was bottle-cap jewels
And a loyalty we didn’t need
[Bridge]
I’d trade the master key to this silent house
For the squeak of that screen door on its hinge
To hear my mom call out that supper’s out
And feel that ache of being truly rich
[Final Chorus]
We were kings of a cardboard castle
Rulers of a two-wheeled steed
Our treasury was bottle-cap jewels
And a loyalty I can’t reseed
